title: epilobium montanum subsp. lanceolatum
intro: Epilobium montanum subsp. lanceolatum, also known as Alpine Willowherb or Mountain Willowherb, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the willowherb family, with lance-shaped leaves and showy, pink to purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help with various health conditions, including urinary tract infections and some types of cancer. The health benefits of Epilobium montanum subsp. lanceolatum include reducing inflammation, acting as an antibacterial agent, and possibly helping to prevent cancer cell growth. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, and can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures.
